metadata.method_name: Model-Diffing M6: shared frozen pt-SAE, co-response unit vs best single latent
metadata.description: Shared Gemma-Scope pt-SAE on gemma-2-2b vs gemma-2-2b-it; detect the RLHF-detox usage shift on toxic inputs, confound bounded by an in-experiment spelling control floor.

member_labels.1920: references to specific biological terms and acronyms
member_labels.14630: character names and terms in various languages, particularly those with diacritics

toxicity.note: AUC>0.5 => unit fires more on toxic than neutral within each model (concept present).

confound.literature_note: Standard model-diffing trains a CROSSCODER (Anthropic 2024); applying a single BASE-trained SAE to IT activations is exactly the misattribution risk Latent-Scaling (arXiv 2504.02922) flags. No gemma-scope-2b-it SAE exists, so this is an INFRASTRUCTURE-BOUNDED diffing result: the spelling control floor (B2) and the reconstruction parity (B1) make that explicit rather than leaving it as future work.

metadata.headline_finding: M6 bounded model-diffing result. The shared BASE-trained Gemma-Scope pt-SAE reconstructs gemma-2-2b-it layer-12 activations almost as well as gemma-2-2b (IT recon cosine=0.913 vs base 0.925; not catastrophic), so the shared-SAE diffing recipe is viable here. A base-vs-IT activation difference IS detectable above the model-label shuffle null for the toxicity unit (AUC=0.438, departure 0.062, direction=it>base). BUT it is NOT concept-specific: the first-letter-spelling CONTROL shows the same direction and a comparable departure (0.062, dir=it>base), and the IT residual-stream norm is 1.11x the base norm. After subtracting the control floor the GENUINE toxicity shift is +0.000 (95% CI [-0.009,0.007]); norm-matched genuine +0.027 (CI [0.021,0.033]). The co-response UNIT does NOT detect the shift more reliably than the best single latent (anchor 12714); abs-dev diff CI [-0.0007237152777777644, 0.007657699652777816]. Note the direction (IT fires the toxicity concept MORE, not less) is opposite the naive detox prediction and is explained by generic OOD/norm drift, not a concept-specific reduction in toxicity-feature usage.
verdict_reasons: toxicity unit shift exceeds shuffle-null 95th pct (AUC_dep=0.062 > 0.005, p=0.0000).
genuine shift CI INCLUDES 0 (0.000, CI[-0.009,0.007]) => shift not separable from generic OOD drift.
IT recon cosine=0.913 (catastrophic<0.5); norm-matched shift survives=True.
